title: Colors
description: hex, rgb, hsl, oklch, and alpha.

questions.prompt: Which color notation lets you tweak lightness intuitively?
questions.explanation: HSL exposes hue, saturation, lightness — easy to tweak by hand.

questions.prompt: Modern CSS color that better matches human perception:
questions.explanation: oklch() (and lab, oklab) are perceptually uniform — great for palettes.
Modern rgb() syntax uses `/` for alpha.
Handy for icon fills that follow text color.

questions.prompt: color-scheme: light dark tells the browser:
options: Support both — use dark form controls in dark mode
questions.explanation: Enables native dark scrollbars/form controls to follow OS preference.

questions.prompt: Best practice for color contrast on text:
options: Aim for WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 for body text)
questions.explanation: Contrast is accessibility, not just taste.
